label: Kalibr cruise missiles — Black Sea to Kyiv
damage: Power grid damage; multiple cities lost electricity
notes: Part of Russia's coordinated mass strike campaign targeting Ukrainian energy infrastructure beginning Oct 2022

label: Shahed-136 drone swarm — Kyiv area
damage: Multiple hits on infrastructure in Kyiv Oblast
notes: One of the largest single-night drone attacks of the war; Ukraine claimed record interception rate

label: Neptune missiles — Moskva flagship sunk
damage: Russian Black Sea Fleet flagship Moskva sunk — largest naval loss since Falklands War

label: Kinzhal hypersonic missiles — Kyiv
damage: None — first confirmed interception of Kinzhal by Patriot
notes: Ukraine's Patriot system intercepted Russia's supposedly 'invincible' Kinzhal missiles, undermining Russian hypersonic propaganda

label: Storm Shadow — Kerch Bridge strike
damage: Significant damage to roadway span of Kerch Bridge; disrupted supply route to Crimea

label: HIMARS — Russian ammunition depot Tokmak
damage: Major ammunition depot destroyed; Russia forced to move supplies 80+ km from front

label: Iskander-M — Zaporizhzhia apartment building
damage: 17 killed, 60+ injured; residential building collapsed

label: Shahed drones — Odesa grain port
damage: Grain storage and port infrastructure damaged; Russia exited grain deal same day
notes: Attack came hours after Russia withdrew from Black Sea Grain Initiative

label: ATACMS — Crimea airfield strike
damage: Russian military helicopters destroyed at Berdiansk; significant damage to Russian aviation assets

label: Kh-101 cruise missiles — Winter 2024 energy strikes
damage: Major hits on Kharkiv, Kyiv, Odesa power substations; widespread blackouts
notes: Part of Russia's sustained winter 2023–24 energy terror campaign

label: Ukrainian drones — Belgorod, Russia
damage: Civilian infrastructure damage in Belgorod; Russia complained to UN
notes: Ukraine targeted Russian border region that serves as base for attacks on Kharkiv Oblast

label: Ukrainian long-range drones — Saratov refinery
damage: Oil refinery damaged; fires burned for hours; Russia's Saratov region 1,200 km from Ukraine

label: Iskander-M — Kramatorsk train station
damage: 59 civilians killed, 100+ injured at crowded train station as evacuees fled east

label: Neptune missiles — Black Sea patrol vessel sunk
damage: Russian patrol vessel Vasily Bekh sunk while resupplying Snake Island garrison

label: Kh-47 Kinzhal — Kharkiv residential areas
damage: Residential buildings struck; 8 killed, 47 injured

label: Storm Shadow — Millerovo airbase (Russia)
damage: Russian military airbase in Rostov Oblast hit; Su-30 aircraft and hangars damaged

label: Largest Shahed drone attack — Aug 2024
damage: Power infrastructure damaged in multiple oblasts; partial blackouts
notes: Russia launched the largest single drone attack of the war; Ukraine claimed record intercepts

label: North Korean KN-23 ballistic missile — Dnipro
damage: Apartment building struck; 18 killed including children; DPRK-supplied missile confirmed by forensic evidence
notes: Confirmed as North Korean KN-23 by US and Ukrainian forensic analysis

label: Ukrainian drones — Kremlin area, Moscow
damage: Minor; drones downed over Kremlin; no injuries; Russia blamed Ukraine; Ukraine denied direct involvement
notes: Ukraine did not officially claim responsibility; some analysts attributed to Ukrainian special operations

label: ATACMS — Saki airbase Crimea
damage: Russian military aircraft destroyed; Saki air base significantly damaged

label: Russia mass missile/drone attack — Ukraine energy infrastructure
damage: Energy infrastructure hit in Zaporizhzhia, Odesa, Poltava, Sumy, Kharkiv, Kherson oblasts; power outages across multiple regions; 4 killed, 24 injured
notes: 392 drones (250 Shahed-type) plus ballistic, Kh-101 and Iskander cruise missiles; Poland scrambled jets to protect airspace; first confirmed destruction of a Zircon launcher in Crimea prevented part of the strike package

label: Ukraine destroys Zircon hypersonic launcher — Crimea (first-ever)
damage: Ground-based Zircon hypersonic missile launcher destroyed while moving into firing position — described as 'first such strike' and testament to Ukraine's maturing operational planning
notes: Ukraine destroyed a Bastion Coastal Missile System in Crimea the following day (Mar 25), the launcher type associated with Zircon missiles

label: Ukrainian drones — Primorsk Baltic oil terminal
damage: Fire at Primorsk oil export facility; cargo loadings halted for at least 36 hours. Part of Ukraine's campaign against Russian energy export infrastructure

label: Ukraine — Ust-Luga Baltic oil port (largest 2026 drone attack)
damage: Novatek oil-product facilities ablaze; storage tanks and loading equipment destroyed; crude loadings halted. Ust-Luga ships ~450,000 bbl/day. Kronstadt naval base area also damaged. ~40% of Russia's oil export capacity halted.
notes: Highest number of drones intercepted in Leningrad region since March 10, 2025; Governor reported 56 intercepted in region alone

label: Russian drone — 14-storey residential building, Dnipro
damage: 14-storey residential building struck; multiple injuries; significant structural damage

label: Ukrainian drones — Ilsky oil refinery, Krasnodar Krai
damage: Ilsky refinery set ablaze; facility processes crude oil for domestic Russian market and military fuel supply chains

label: Russian guided aerial bombs — Kharkiv city
damage: At least 11 civilians killed; multiple residential and civilian infrastructure sites hit in Kharkiv city

label: Russian Shahed drone swarm — Odesa (maternity hospital hit)
damage: Maternity hospital roof struck; 3 educational institutions damaged; apartment buildings damaged; 2 killed, 11 injured including 9-year-old boy; 81 evacuated

label: Ukrainian long-range drones — Slavneft-YANOS refinery, Yaroslavl
damage: Fires at Slavneft-YANOS refinery (300,000 bpd; Rosneft/Gazprom); 1 child killed, 3 injured near strike site; ~700 km from Ukrainian border

label: Ukrainian drones — Leningrad Oblast oil terminals (3rd consecutive night)
damage: Oil terminal infrastructure in Leningrad Oblast struck for third straight night; sustained pressure on Russia's Baltic oil export capacity following March 25 Ust-Luga and March 27 Primorsk strikes

label: Russia 442-drone mass attack — Ukraine nationwide (Mar 29)
damage: 4 killed (2 in Odesa, 1 Kryvyi Rih, 1 Poltava); 13+ wounded in Odesa; port infrastructure hit; Ozernoye Airbase in Zhytomyr region struck by 7 Geran-2 drones; largest single-night drone barrage of the week

label: Ukrainian drones — Ust-Luga Baltic port (4th consecutive night)
damage: Fresh fires at Ust-Luga fertilizer, oil, and coal export hub; new damage confirmed by regional governor; cumulative campaign has disrupted up to 40% of Russian Baltic oil export capacity since March 23

label: Ukrainian drones — Taganrog, Rostov Oblast (1 killed)
damage: 1 killed, several wounded in Taganrog; dozens of residential buildings damaged; part of Ukraine's ongoing deep-strike campaign targeting Russian-territory border regions

label: Russia daily drone/airstrike barrage — Ukraine frontline and cities
damage: 147 combat engagements; 70 airstrikes, 237 guided aerial bombs, sustained drone attacks targeting frontline positions and energy infrastructure across multiple oblasts
notes: 9,355 total kamikaze drones deployed including short-range FPV drones along frontline; Russian advance continued near Hulyaipole in Zaporizhzhia Oblast

label: Ukrainian drones — Ust-Luga Baltic port (5th consecutive night)
damage: Fresh fires at Ust-Luga oil and chemical export hub; cumulative 5-night campaign (March 23–April 1) estimated to have disrupted ~40% of Russian Baltic oil export capacity

label: Russian drone strike — Nikopol, Dnipropetrovsk Oblast
damage: 11 people injured including a child; infrastructure damage in Nikopol city

label: Ukrainian strikes — 3 radar stations, Crimea
damage: Three Russian radar stations in Crimea struck; part of Ukraine's ongoing campaign to degrade Russia's air defense and early-warning network

label: Russian drone strike — Chuhuiv residential building, Kharkiv Oblast
damage: Residential building struck in Chuhuiv; fires on two floors; 3 women injured. Additional drone strike in Kyivskyi district of Kharkiv city set vehicles ablaze; 2 people injured by shelling in Shevchenkivskyi district

label: Russian drone and artillery barrage — Dnipropetrovsk Oblast (10+ strikes)
damage: Over 10 separate strikes across Dnipropetrovsk Oblast; 1 person killed, 2 injured; infrastructure damage reported

label: Ukrainian drones — 'Strela' cruise missile component factory, Bryansk
damage: Manufacturing components facility for 'Strela' cruise missiles struck in Bryansk region; part of Ukraine's campaign to degrade Russian defense-industrial missile production capacity

label: Russian ballistic missiles — Kharkiv residential buildings
damage: 4-story residential building fire in Osnovianskyi district; 1 woman injured; Kharkiv has sustained 20+ drone/missile strikes in early April alone

label: Russian drone — Sumy shopping center
damage: Shopping center in Sumy city center struck; 4 people injured; civilian commercial area targeted

label: Ukraine strikes Kirovske airfield — occupied Crimea
damage: Ukrainian military intelligence struck Kirovske military airfield in occupied Crimea; part of ongoing campaign to degrade Russian air assets and infrastructure in the peninsula

label: Ukrainian drones — Novorossiysk Black Sea energy hub
damage: Black Sea energy export hub at Novorossiysk struck; part of Ukraine's sustained campaign to degrade Russian energy and logistics infrastructure following Baltic port strikes at Ust-Luga and Primorsk in late March 2026
notes: Expands Ukraine's energy infrastructure campaign from Baltic ports and inland refineries to Black Sea export terminals

label: Russia mass missile & drone attack — 'Easter escalation' (14+ civilians killed)
damage: 14+ civilians killed, dozens wounded across multiple Ukrainian cities; Zelensky condemns 'Easter escalation'; second wave kills at least 5 more
notes: Combined missile and drone barrage targeting residential areas and civilian infrastructure; strikes continue Russia's attritional campaign ahead of Orthodox Easter (April 20)

label: Russian drone — Odesa port critical infrastructure
damage: Critical infrastructure at Odesa port damaged; maritime export operations disrupted

label: Russia deadliest aerial barrage in months — 18 killed across Ukraine
damage: 18 civilians killed including a 12-year-old girl; multiple cities struck; energy and civilian infrastructure hit across several oblasts
notes: Described by Ukrainian officials as the deadliest single aerial attack in months; coordinated missiles and drone barrage

label: Ukrainian long-range drones — Tuapse oil refinery 'volcano' (1st strike)
damage: Major fires at Tuapse refinery (~12M tons/year capacity); described as turning facility into a 'volcano'; part of escalating campaign against Russian Black Sea energy infrastructure

label: Ukrainian drones — Tuapse refinery 2nd strike in one week, major fire at tank farm
damage: Second strike on Tuapse refinery in one week; major fire at tank farm; 1 killed, 1 injured; confirmed by Ukrainian General Staff as joint USF, Special Operations, and HUR operation

label: Russia 143-drone evening barrage — Ukraine (116 shot down)
damage: 1,354 attacks on Donetsk region damaging 30 civilian facilities; 12 injured in Kharkiv region; educational institution destroyed in Sloviansk (3 injured); medical facility hit in Sumy (4 injured)

label: Ukrainian Neptune missiles — Atlant Aero drone factory, Taganrog
damage: Two production facilities destroyed, four others damaged at Atlant Aero plant in Taganrog. The facility manufactures Molniya strike-reconnaissance UAVs and Orion drone components used in Russian strikes on Ukraine. Confirmed by Ukrainian General Staff and satellite imagery on April 24.
notes: Confirmed April 24 by Ukrainian Navy + satellite imagery. At least third strike on this facility (previous hits: January 2026, April 19). Russian MoD claimed air defense intercepted a Neptune missile in the period.

label: Russian Shahed drones + ballistic missiles — Odesa (elderly couple killed, merchant ship hit)
damage: 2 killed (elderly couple), 15 injured; 3 residential buildings on fire; 36 residents evacuated; Saint Kitts and Nevis-flagged bulk carrier struck in Black Sea shipping corridor (no crew casualties among 22 crew)
notes: 107 Shahed-type drones + 2 ballistic missiles launched. 96 drones destroyed/jammed by Ukrainian air defenses; 10 drones and both ballistic missiles hit targets. Merchant vessel struck in Odesa maritime corridor.

label: Russia 400+ Shahed drone swarm — Dnipro (5 killed, building destroyed, 12+ hr attack)
damage: 4-story residential building destroyed; industrial facility and gas station struck (3 trucks burned); 5 killed, 30+ injured in Dnipro; 23 hospitalized. City under attack for 12+ consecutive hours.
notes: Component of 666-asset mass attack. ~400 Shahed-type kamikaze drones launched from occupied territory/Crimea direction, part of 619 total drones. Air defenses intercepted 580 of 619 drones (94%).

label: Russian cruise + ballistic missiles — Kharkiv, Nizhyn (7 killed total)
damage: Kharkiv: Kyivskyi and Shevchenkivskyi districts struck, public transport stop destroyed, gas pipeline damaged; 1.5-year-old child suffered acute stress. Chernihiv Oblast/Nizhyn: 2 killed, 2 injured from drone and rocket attack.
notes: 47 total missiles: 35 cruise missiles (Kh-101, Kalibr) + 12 ballistic (Iskander-M). Air defenses intercepted 30 of 47. Part of 666-asset mass overnight attack on April 25. Romania's NATO territory hit by debris — first physical property damage (electricity pole, annex in Galati). RAF Typhoon shot down 1 Russian UAV near Romanian-Ukrainian border: first NATO aircraft kill of a Russian drone.

label: Russian guided aerial bombs — Zaporizhzhia Oblast, Huliaipole direction (1 killed)
damage: Civilian minibus struck near Huliaipole, killing 1 and injuring 4; additional strikes on settlements of Verkhnia Tersa, Kopani, Shyroke, Huliaipilske, Novoselivka; 18 combat engagements in Huliaipole direction as Russian forces advance west of Gulyaypole
notes: Russia advancing from Mirnoye; expanded control near Vozdvizhevka. Chernobyl 40th anniversary date; ISW confirmed 149 total combat engagements across Ukraine on April 26.

label: Ukrainian long-range drones — Yaroslavl refinery (second strike, April 2026)
damage: Slavneft-YANOS refinery struck again — facility processes 15M tons/year; fires reported. Second strike on this target in April 2026 (first: March 28). Simultaneous with PhosAgro fertilizer plant strike in northwest Russia.
notes: Part of Ukraine's strategic deep-strike campaign against Russian energy and industrial capacity; Tuapse refinery also still burning from prior strikes.

label: Ukrainian drones — PhosAgro fertilizer plant, Cherepovets (second attack in April 2026)
damage: PhosAgro PJSC fertilizer plant struck for second time in April 2026; Bloomberg confirmed strike; plant is one of Russia's largest fertilizer producers and key foreign exchange earner
notes: Bloomberg confirmed; part of Ukraine's broader campaign to degrade Russia's agricultural export revenue and industrial complex.

label: Russian Shahed drones — Odesa residential buildings and hotel (11 injured)
damage: High-rise residential buildings and hotel struck; 11 injured including 2 children
notes: Part of overnight Russian drone campaign targeting Odesa region; air defenses engaged but drones penetrated coverage in residential areas.

label: Russian Shahed drones — Chornomorsk port, ~6,000-ton sunflower oil spill in Black Sea
damage: Seaport infrastructure and storage tank struck; ~6,000 tons of sunflower oil spilled into Black Sea — major environmental disaster for the region
notes: Chornomorsk is one of Ukraine's main Black Sea export ports (~30 km SW of Odesa). Oil spill poses major environmental risk to the Black Sea ecosystem. Simultaneous with Odesa residential attacks.
